content: Let me start by reading the necessary configuration files and then audit the specified Lean files.
I'll read the auditor instructions and project context, then examine the specified files.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (iter-202 Lane TS — file-skeleton scaffold)
38	
39	This file is the **iter-202 Lane TS** file-skeleton: each of the 4 pinned
40	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
41	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`) with a
42	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-203+ work: the `tensorObj` definition lifts
43	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification, and the consumer
44	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` then closes the residual `addCommGroup`
45	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.
46	
47	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
48	
49	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
50	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
51	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
52	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.

56	   functorial action of `⊗` on morphisms: a pair `f : M ⟶ M'`, `g : N ⟶ N'`
57	   determines `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`.
58	   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.
59	
60	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` (instance) — the
61	   monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X` with tensor `⊗`, unit
62	   `O_X`, associator, unitors, and braiding inherited from
63	   `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification.
64	   Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`.
65	
66	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (def) — the
67	   `AddCommGroup` structure on the relative Picard quotient
68	   `Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)`, built via the
69	   `tensorObj` substrate. This is the iter-204+ closure target for the Lane RPF
70	   L235/L266-269 `addCommGroup` residual sorry.
71	   Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`.
72	
73	Plus (PUSH-BEYOND) the supporting helper lemmas of the lift section
74	(`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`,
75	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`,
76	`lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj`).

81	4 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
82	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tags 01CR (Picard group), 03DM (relative
83	tensor product of `O_X`-modules). Mathlib module
84	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules`
85	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`).

88	set_option autoImplicit false
89	
90	universe u
91	
92	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory
93	
94	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
95	
96	namespace Scheme
97	
98	namespace Modules
99	
100	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
101	
102	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
103	
104	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
105	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
106	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
107	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
108	
109	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
110	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
111	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
112	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
113	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
114	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
115	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
116	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
117	
118	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
119	
120	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
121	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
122	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
123	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
124	
125	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
126	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
127	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
128	sheafification. -/
129	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
130	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
131	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
132	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
133	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
134	
135	/-! ## §2. The monoidal-category structure -/
136	
137	/-- **Monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
138	
139	The bifunctor `⊗_X` of `tensorObj_functoriality`, with unit object the
140	structure sheaf `O_X`, associator `α`, left/right unitors `λ`, `ρ`, and
141	braiding `β` inherited from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification,
142	satisfies the pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms; hence `Scheme.Modules X`
143	carries a canonical (symmetric) monoidal-category structure.
144	
145	Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
146	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body discharges the coherence axioms
147	affine-locally (each reduces to the analogous identity for the tensor product
148	of modules over a commutative ring) and assembles the
149	`CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory` instance. -/
150	noncomputable instance monoidalCategory {X : Scheme.{u}} :
151	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) :=
152	  sorry
153	
154	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
155	
156	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
157	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
158	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
159	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
160	
161	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
162	
163	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
164	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
165	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
166	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
167	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
168	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
169	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
170	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
171	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
172	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
173	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
174	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
175	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
176	
177	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
178	sheaf.**
179	
180	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
181	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
182	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
183	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
184	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
185	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
186	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
187	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
188	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
189	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
190	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
191	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
192	
193	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
194	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
195	
196	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
197	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
198	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
199	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
200	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
201	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
202	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
203	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
204	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
205	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
206	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
207	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
208	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
209	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
210	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
211	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
212	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
213	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
214	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
215	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
216	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
217	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
218	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
219	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
220	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
221	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
222	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
223	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
224	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
225	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
226	
227	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
228	
229	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
230	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
231	
232	This is the single missing-infrastructure ingredient of `A.1.c.SubT`. It says
233	the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor) commutes
234	with the restriction functor `restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` along an open
235	immersion. Mathematically it factors as: (i) the presheaf-of-modules tensor
236	commutes with the pullback of presheaves (sectionwise this is the
237	extension-of-scalars identity
238	`B ⊗_A (P ⊗_A Q) ≅ (B ⊗_A P) ⊗_B (B ⊗_A Q)` for `A → B`, Stacks 03DM); and
239	(ii) sheafification commutes with pullback along an open immersion (the
240	restriction is exact and the small-Zariski sheafification is local). Neither is
241	in Mathlib at the `SheafOfModules` level (there is no monoidal structure on
242	`SheafOfModules`, hence no strong-monoidal pullback). A genuine construction
243	requires either the `Localization.Monoidal` transport of the monoidal structure
244	through `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization` together with
245	`W.IsMonoidal` for `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)` (an instance NOT
246	present in Mathlib and the real residual obstacle), or a direct sectionwise
247	construction. Left as a named typed `sorry` feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`;
248	see the task result for the precise missing instance statement. -/
249	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
250	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
251	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
252	  -- Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
253	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
254	  -- Remaining genuine infrastructure goal: the pullback functor is strong
255	  -- monoidal for the substrate `⊗`, i.e.
256	  -- `(pullback f).obj (M ⊗ N) ≅ (pullback f).obj M ⊗ (pullback f).obj N`
257	  -- (here `(pullback f).obj M ≃ M.restrict f` again via
258	  -- `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`). At the underlying-presheaf level this is the
259	  -- extension-of-scalars identity `B ⊗_A (P ⊗_A Q) ≅ (B ⊗_A P) ⊗_B (B ⊗_A Q)`
260	  -- composed with the fact that sheafification commutes with pullback along an
261	  -- open immersion. Neither half exists at the `SheafOfModules` level in
262	  -- Mathlib (no monoidal structure ⇒ no strong-monoidal pullback). See the
263	  -- docstring above and the task result for the precise missing ingredient.
264	  sorry
265	
266	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
267	
268	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
269	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
270	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
271	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
272	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
273	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
274	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
275	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
276	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
277	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
278	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
279	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
280	  intro x
281	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
282	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
283	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
284	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
285	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
286	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
287	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
288	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
289	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
290	    tensorObj_unit_iso
291	
292	/-- **Inverse of an invertible module.**
293	
294	Every line bundle `L : X.Modules` has a two-sided tensor inverse: there is a
295	locally-trivial `Linv : X.Modules` (the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)`) together with
296	a tensor isomorphism `L ⊗_X Linv ≅ 𝟙_ (X.Modules)`. Per blueprint
297	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`;
298	the iter-203+ body builds the dual and the contraction isomorphism, which is an
299	isomorphism affine-locally on a trivialising cover. -/
300	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
301	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
302	    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
303	      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ 𝟙_ (X.Modules)) :=
304	  sorry
305	
306	/-- **Restriction of `⊗` to the `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier.**
307	
308	For `S`-schemes `C, T`, the bifunctor `⊗_{C ×_S T}` restricts to the subtype
309	`LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` of locally-trivial modules on `C ×_S T`, with unit
310	the structure sheaf and the dual as two-sided inverse. Per blueprint
311	`lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`. -/
312	noncomputable def tensorObjOnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S)
313	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
314	  ⟨tensorObj L.carrier L'.carrier,
315	    tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial L.isLocallyTrivial L'.isLocallyTrivial⟩
316	
317	end Modules

350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — monoidal sheafification of presheaves of modules
351	
352	This section records the **verified axiom-clean decomposition** of the single
353	infrastructure obstacle blocking `Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` and
354	`Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso` (see `§2` and `§3` above): the absence,
355	at Mathlib's pinned commit, of a `MonoidalCategory` structure on
356	`SheafOfModules R` for the *relative* module tensor `⊗_R`.
357	
358	The blueprint proof of `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal` (chapter
359	`Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`) transports the monoidal structure through the
360	sheafification localization
361	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification α : PresheafOfModules R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules R`,
362	which is an `IsLocalization` for
363	`W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)`
364	(Mathlib: `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization`,
365	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization`). The generic
366	localization-monoidal transport
367	`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal`
368	produces the wanted `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R)` from this localization
369	*together with* a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal W` instance.
370	
371	The two declarations below close the **entire** transport pipeline
372	axiom-clean, reducing the whole substrate to the single residual fact
373	`whiskerLeft` (`W g → W (F ◁ g)`), which is the genuine Mathlib-absent
374	ingredient. They are stated at full generality (any site `J`, any presheaf of
375	commutative rings `R'`, any sheaf of rings `R` with the standard
376	locally-bijective/sheafify instances) so the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal
377	instance fires syntactically.
378	
379	* `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft` builds `W.IsMonoidal` from `whiskerLeft` alone:
380	  `IsMultiplicative W` is automatic (an `inverseImage` of the multiplicative
381	  localizing property `J.W`), and `whiskerRight` follows from `whiskerLeft` via
382	  the symmetric braiding on `PresheafOfModules` (the `arrow_mk_iso_iff` trick of
383	  `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerRight`).
384	* `monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW` is the transport proper: given
385	  `[W.IsMonoidal]`, it produces `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R)` via
386	  `instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal` with unit iso `Iso.refl`.
387	
388	**Residual gap (precise).** The sole remaining fact is `whiskerLeft`:
389	for `g` inverted by sheafification (i.e. `W g`, equivalently `g` locally
390	bijective) and any `F`, the relative tensor `F ◁ g` is again inverted by
391	sheafification. The slick Mathlib proof for the *ambient* (cartesian / `⊗_ℤ`)
392	tensor on `Sheaf J A` (`CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`)
393	goes through `MonoidalClosed A` and "the internal hom of a sheaf is a sheaf".
394	The analogue here requires `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` for the
395	relative `⊗_R` — **not present in Mathlib** (the closed structure on
396	`ModuleCat R` does not lift to presheaves of modules over a varying ring). That
397	closed-monoidal infrastructure (the module analogue of
398	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`) is the genuine multi-file blocker. -/
399	section MathlibSupplementMonoidalSheafification
400	
401	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory PresheafOfModules Localization.Monoidal
402	
403	universe v' u'
404	
405	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
406	  {R' : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{v'}}
407	
408	/-- **`W.IsMonoidal` reduces to `whiskerLeft`.** For the module-sheafification
409	localization property `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf (R' ⋙ forget₂ _ _))`,
410	the `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal W` structure follows from the single hypothesis
411	that `W` is stable under left whiskering (`W g → W (F ◁ g)`): the multiplicative
412	part is automatic, and the right-whiskering part follows from the symmetric
413	braiding on `PresheafOfModules`. Project-local: this is the precise, compiled
414	decomposition of the `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal` obstruction down to its lone
415	Mathlib-absent ingredient. -/
416	@[implicit_reducible]
417	noncomputable def isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft
418	    (hwl : ∀ ⦃G₁ G₂ : PresheafOfModules (R' ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⦄
419	        {g : G₁ ⟶ G₂}, ((J.W (A := AddCommGrpCat)).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)) g →
420	        ∀ (F : PresheafOfModules (R' ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)),
421	          ((J.W (A := AddCommGrpCat)).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)) (F ◁ g)) :
422	    ((J.W (A := AddCommGrpCat)).inverseImage
423	      (toPresheaf (R' ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).IsMonoidal where
424	  whiskerLeft F _ _ g hg := hwl hg F
425	  whiskerRight {F₁ F₂} f hf G :=
426	    (((J.W (A := AddCommGrpCat)).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)).arrow_mk_iso_iff
427	      (Arrow.isoMk (β_ F₁ G) (β_ F₂ G))).2 (hwl hf G)
428	
429	variable {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'}} (α : (R' ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶ R.obj)
430	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
431	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}]
432	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}]
433	
434	/-- **Localization-monoidal transport for sheaves of modules.** Given a
435	`MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal W` instance for the module-sheafification
436	localization property `W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)`, the category
437	`SheafOfModules R` inherits a `MonoidalCategory` structure, transported from the
438	relative module tensor on `PresheafOfModules R₀` through the sheafification
439	localization functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification α`
440	(`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal`
441	with unit iso `Iso.refl`). Project-local: this closes the transport half of
442	`thm:scheme_modules_monoidal` axiom-clean; combined with
443	`isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft` it reduces `Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` to
444	exactly the `whiskerLeft` ingredient. -/
445	@[implicit_reducible]
446	noncomputable def monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW
447	    [((J.W (A := AddCommGrpCat)).inverseImage
448	        (toPresheaf (R' ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).IsMonoidal] :
449	    MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules R) :=
450	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
451	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := sheafification α)
452	      (W := (J.W).inverseImage (toPresheaf _)) (Iso.refl _)))
453	
454	end MathlibSupplementMonoidalSheafification
455

107	## Note on type expressivity
108	
109	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
110	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
111	
112	- `addCommGroup` is an `AddCommGroup` instance on a quotient set whose
113	  underlying type is itself a typed `sorry` in A.1.b (`OnProduct` carrier);
114	  the substantive content is the existence of the group structure
115	  (closure under `+`, inverse, neutral element), which is non-trivial
116	  even at the level of typed-`sorry` carriers — once the carrier is
117	  unpacked, the group operations are induced by the tensor product on
118	  invertible sheaves modulo the subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
119	
120	- `PicSharp` returns a contravariant *functor*, not a constant family;
121	  the object and morphism actions are independently substantive (the
122	  morphism action is the set-valued `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of
123	  A.1.b lifted to a group homomorphism).
124	
125	- `PicSharp.functorial` returns an `AddMonoidHom`, not a bare set map;
126	  this is a strict strengthening of the set-valued version in A.1.b.
127	
128	- `PicSharp.presheaf` returns a `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`,
129	  which on objects matches `PicSharp` and on morphisms matches
130	  `PicSharp.functorial` — substantive content is the assembly into a
131	  single category-theoretic object.
132	
133	- `PicSharp.etSheaf` returns a `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` for the given
134	  topology `J`; the sheafification is non-trivial because `PicSharp`
135	  is generally not even a Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2, L1292–L1302).

188	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
189	
190	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
191	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
192	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
193	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
194	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
195	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
196	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
197	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
198	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
199	
200	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
201	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
202	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
203	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
204	
205	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Kleiman §2,
206	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01CR). -/
207	
208	/-- **Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient.**
209	
210	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test
211	object `πT : T ⟶ S`, the quotient set
212	```
213	Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)
214	  = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
215	```
216	carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the descent of
217	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, the zero element is the class
218	`[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (the dual line
219	bundle). Well-definedness on the quotient uses the subgroup property of
220	`π_T^* Pic(T)` from `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`.
221	
222	iter-198 refresh: A.1.b's `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier WAS closed
223	in `LineBundlePullback.lean` iter-188 (concretised as
224	`{ M : (pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }`). The
225	remaining gate is *not* on the carrier but on the *tensor-product*
226	`AddCommGroup` structure on `LineBundle.OnProduct`: forming the
227	abelian-group law `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` requires a tensor product
228	on the underlying carrier, and Mathlib at the pinned commit
229	`b80f227` ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but does not
230	expose a monoidal-category structure on the higher-level
231	`Scheme.Modules` category (cf. `LineBundlePullback.lean` L344--L346).
232	This is the upstream-Mathlib gate referred to as the
233	"`Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure gap" in the chapter file's
234	``Gate annotation (iter-198 refresh)'' paragraph. -/
235	noncomputable instance addCommGroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
236	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
237	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := by
238	  -- iter-198 Lane RPF gate annotation: the residual gate is the
239	  -- tensor-product `AddCommGroup` structure on
240	  -- `LineBundle.OnProduct`, NOT the `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier
241	  -- itself (which was concretised iter-188).
242	  --
243	  -- Intended construction once the Mathlib `Scheme.Modules`
244	  -- monoidal-structure gap is closed:
245	  --   1. `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` carries `AddCommGroup` (tensor product
246	  --      on invertible sheaves; Stacks tag 01CR, Hartshorne II §6: unit is the
247	  --      structure sheaf `O_{C×_S T}`, inverse is the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O)`).
248	  --      MISSING at Mathlib b80f227: no monoidal structure on
249	  --      `Scheme.Modules`; only `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` is
250	  --      shipped, one level below.
251	  --   2. `pullbackHom : T.LineBundle →+ LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` is the
252	  --      additive structure underlying `LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT`
253	  --      (preserves zero: `π_T^* O_T ≅ O_{C×_S T}`; preserves addition: tensor
254	  --      compatibility of pullback `π_T^*(N ⊗ N') ≅ π_T^* N ⊗ π_T^* N'`).
255	  --      Let `H := pullbackHom.range : AddSubgroup (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)`.
256	  --   3. The Setoid `preimage_subgroup πC πT` (currently iso-class equivalence
257	  --      `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)`) coincides with
258	  --      `QuotientAddGroup.leftRel H` (both encode `L ~ L' ↔ L⁻¹ ⊗ L' ∈ H`)
259	  --      once tensor product and inverse are concrete; hence
260	  --      `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) ≃ LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT ⧸ H`.
261	  --   4. The RHS has the canonical `AddCommGroup` from
262	  --      `QuotientAddGroup.Quotient.addCommGroup`; transport along the Equiv of
263	  --      (3) via `Equiv.addCommGroup` (Mathlib.Algebra.Group.TransferInstance)
264	  --      yields the desired instance on the LHS.
265	  --
266	  -- TODO (Scheme.Modules monoidal-structure gate): close once Mathlib
267	  -- ships a monoidal-category instance on `Scheme.Modules` (or once
268	  -- the project-side `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` lemma lands).
269	  exact sorry
270	
271	end PicSharp

273	/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor
274	
275	We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
276	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
277	instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor
278	
279	```
280	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
281	```
282	
283	sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
284	`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
285	morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
286	`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
287	line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.
288	
289	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
290	
291	/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
292	integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor
293	
294	```
295	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
296	```
297	
298	On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
299	quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
300	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).
301	
302	On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
303	`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
304	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
305	`PicSharp.functorial` below).
306	
307	Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
308	underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
309	`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
310	`Type (u+1)`).
311	
312	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body is a `Functor.const`-style trivial
313	functor at `AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit.{u+2} : AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`. This
314	is a sorry-free placeholder used while the file-local `addCommGroup`
315	sorry in §1 is open: the *intended* construction
316	(`obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))`
317	with `map := PicSharp.functorial`) requires the AddCommGroup
318	operations on the quotient to be available concretely to verify the
319	functor identity and composition laws, which is gated on the Mathlib
320	`Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure upgrade (Section
321	``Gate annotation (iter-198 refresh)'' of the chapter). The trivial
322	target is harmless: downstream consumers (`PicSharp.presheaf`,
323	`PicSharp.etSheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf_group_structure`) work with the
324	*group-valued* presheaf signature, not with the specific assignment on
325	objects, so this placeholder unblocks the file-skeleton and lets the
326	sheafification machinery elaborate axiom-cleanly. -/
327	noncomputable def PicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
328	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
329	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
330	  (CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of (PUnit.{u+2}))
331	
332	namespace PicSharp
333	
334	/-! ## §3. Functoriality (group-homomorphism strengthening)
335	
336	The naturality lemma `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of A.1.b produces, for
337	each morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, a set map
338	```
339	g^♯ : Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T) ⟶ Pic(C ×_S T') / π_{T'}^* Pic(T')
340	```
341	of quotient sets. Combined with the abelian-group instance of §1 on
342	both sides, this set map upgrades to an additive-monoid homomorphism
343	`AddMonoidHom`: indeed `g_C^*` preserves tensor products and the
344	structure sheaf, so it preserves the abelian-group operations on both
345	sides; the upgrade is the substantive content of
346	`lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial`.
347	
348	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` (Kleiman §2,
349	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
350	
351	/-- **Functoriality of the relative Picard presheaf, group-hom form.**
352	
353	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects
354	`πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
355	(encoded by `πT' = g ≫ πT`), the set map
356	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial πC πT πT' g hg` upgrades to an
357	`AddMonoidHom`-homomorphism with respect to the abelian-group structure
358	of `PicSharp.addCommGroup` on source and target.
359	
360	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body is the zero AddMonoidHom. The
361	`AddMonoidHom.zero` instance is provided whenever the codomain has an
362	`AddCommGroup`, which is supplied by the file-local `addCommGroup`
363	instance of §1 (currently with a sorry body, gated on the Mathlib
364	`Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure upgrade). The math-correct
365	construction — wrapping `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` with `map_zero` and
366	`map_add` proofs from `Scheme.Modules.pullback` preserving the
367	structure sheaf and tensor products — needs the AddCommGroup
368	operations on the quotient to be concrete (so that the proof goals
369	`f 0 = 0` and `f (a + b) = f a + f b` can be discharged at the level
370	of representatives). This is gated on the same upstream Mathlib
371	upgrade. -/
372	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
373	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
374	    (_hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
375	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →+
376	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
377	  0
378	
379	/-! ## §4. Wrapping as a functor instance
380	
381	The group-valued presheaf bundling: combine the on-objects assignment
382	of `PicSharp` (carrying the `addCommGroup` structure) with the
383	on-morphisms assignment of `functorial` (each a group hom) into a
384	single functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. The identity /
385	composition laws of the functor are inherited from the corresponding
386	identities on `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, descended through the
387	quotient.
388	
389	This wrapper is kept distinct from `PicSharp` to mirror the blueprint
390	split (`def:rel_pic_sharp` vs.\ `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`) and to
391	record the explicit re-packaging step from the lemma-by-lemma data
392	(`addCommGroup`, `PicSharp`, `functorial`) into a single
393	category-theoretic functor — useful when applying functorial
394	constructions (Yoneda, sheafification, …) to the *bundled* form.
395	
396	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` (Kleiman §2,
397	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
398	
399	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf, bundled.**
400	
401	The relative Picard functor `PicSharp_{C/k}` packaged as a single
402	contravariant functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, with object
403	action `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (group structure
404	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`) and morphism action `g ↦ g^♯` (group hom from
405	`PicSharp.functorial`). Identity and composition laws are inherited
406	from `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` / `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`
407	descended through the quotient.
408	
409	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body re-exports `PicSharp _C` (the
410	on-objects/on-morphisms data of §2 already produces a functor into
411	`AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`). The chapter splits the "object/morphism
412	description" (`PicSharp`) and the "bundled functor" (`presheaf`) to
413	mirror the blueprint partition `def:rel_pic_sharp` /
414	`thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`; on the Lean side they are the same data,
415	so `presheaf := PicSharp _C` is a clean closure.
416	
417	Note on naming: this is the canonical "fully assembled" form mentioned
418	as `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` in the blueprint; it is structurally a
419	`def` (not a `theorem`) because the conclusion is a `Functor` (data),
420	not a `Prop`. -/
421	noncomputable def presheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
422	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
423	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
424	  PicSharp _C
425	
426	end PicSharp
427	
428	/-! ## §5. Étale sheafification
429	
430	The relative Picard presheaf `PicSharp_{C/k}` is generally not even a
431	Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2 L1292–L1302). To obtain a representable
432	functor in the sense of `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`
433	(downstream A.2.c), one replaces `PicSharp_{C/k}` by its **associated
434	étale sheaf** `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{∼ét}`. Kleiman §4
435	(Theorem `th:main`) represents precisely this sheafified functor.
436	
437	For the iter-176 file-skeleton, we encode the sheafification as
438	`presheafToSheaf J _` applied to the bundled `PicSharp.presheaf` for a
439	**parameter** Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` — because
440	Mathlib at the pinned commit does not ship an étale Grothendieck
441	topology on schemes (it ships only the morphism property
442	`AlgebraicGeometry.Etale`). iter-177+ refinement: bind `J` to the
443	canonical étale topology once it lands in Mathlib.
444	
445	*Naming note*: the blueprint pins this declaration under
446	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`, which already names the
447	*representing scheme* in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`. We use
448	the namespaced name `PicSharp.etSheaf` here; the blueprint will be
449	updated by the plan/review agents.
450	
451	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification` (Kleiman §2,
452	`df:Pfs` étale-sheaf clause). -/
453	
454	/-- The **étale sheafification of the relative Picard presheaf**.
455	
456	Given a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C` over a field
457	`k`, and a Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` (intended: the
458	canonical étale topology on `(Sch/k)`), the **étale-sheafified relative
459	Picard functor**
460	
461	```
462	Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{~_ét}
463	```
464	
465	is the sheafification of the group-valued presheaf
466	`PicSharp.presheaf` of `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`. Equivalently, it is
467	the unique (up to canonical isomorphism) sheaf of abelian groups on the
468	site `(Over (Spec k), J)` equipped with a presheaf morphism
469	`PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (forget _) ∘ -` universal among presheaf morphisms
470	to abelian-group sheaves.
471	
472	Encoded as a `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` object; iter-177+ implementations
473	will fix `J` to the étale topology and unfold via
474	`CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf` (Mathlib's
475	`Sites.ConcreteSheafification`). The body of the iter-176 file-skeleton
476	is a typed `sorry`.
477	
478	In Kleiman's notation with `X = C` and `S = Spec k`, this is
479	`Pic_{(X/S)ét}` from `df:Pfs`.
480	
481	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: body is
482	`(CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).obj (PicSharp.presheaf _C)`.
483	The Mathlib `HasWeakSheafify` instance for any Grothendieck topology
484	with abelian-group target is supplied by
485	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification`. -/
486	noncomputable def PicSharp.etSheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
487	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom]
488	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
489	    Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
490	  (CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}).obj (PicSharp.presheaf _C)
491	
492	namespace PicSharp
493	
494	/-! ## §6. Sheafification preserves the abelian-group structure
495	
496	The étale sheafification of an abelian-group-valued presheaf is itself
497	abelian-group-valued: the forgetful functor `AddCommGrpCat ⥤ Type`
498	preserves filtered colimits (these are computed pointwise), and the
499	plus-construction is built from filtered colimits, so sheafification
500	commutes with the forgetful functor up to canonical isomorphism. The
501	substantive content is the **sheafification unit**: a canonical
502	morphism of presheaves `PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf ?).obj`
503	(in `AddCommGrpCat`) that exhibits the universal property of
504	sheafification.
505	
506	This statement does NOT have an explicit `\lean{...}` pin in the
507	blueprint (`thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` is a description
508	rather than a Lean target); we surface it here so the downstream
509	representability statement (`chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`) can
510	cite the unit directly.
511	
512	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` (Kleiman
513	§2, `df:Pfs`; standard `Sheafification.toSheafify` content). -/
514	
515	/-- **Sheafification unit for the étale Picard sheafification.**
516	
517	Given the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor
518	`PicSharp.etSheaf C J : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`, there is a canonical
519	morphism of (group-valued) presheaves
520	```
521	η_C : PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj
522	```
523	in the functor category `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`.
524	
525	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: we witness the `Nonempty` via the zero
526	natural transformation between presheaves of abelian groups; both
527	`PicSharp.presheaf C` and `(PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj` have target
528	`AddCommGrpCat`, which has zero morphisms, hence the functor category
529	also has zero morphisms (computed pointwise). This satisfies the
530	`Nonempty` claim of the theorem statement without depending on the
531	file-local `addCommGroup` sorry body in §1 (the zero morphism between
532	abelian-group-valued presheaves exists unconditionally on the
533	typeclass `AddCommGroup` on values; the `addCommGroup` instance is
534	only used to ensure the *target* category is `AddCommGrpCat` for the
535	sheafification step). The richer statement — that this morphism is
536	the universal sheafification unit `toSheafify` — is gated on the
537	Mathlib `Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure upgrade that closes the
538	`addCommGroup` sorry in §1. -/
539	theorem etSheaf_group_structure {k : Type u} [Field k]
540	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
541	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
542	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
543	    Nonempty (PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj) :=
544	  ⟨0⟩
545	
546	end PicSharp
547	
548	end Scheme
549	
550	end AlgebraicGeometry
551

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry
95

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
